question: The P^2 nematic lattice model has site variables hat{n}_r in P^2 (three-dimensional unit vectors with sign identification hat{n} <-> -hat{n}) and nearest-neighbor interaction Hamiltonian H = -J * sum_{<r,r'>} (1/2)[3(hat{n}_r . hat{n}_{r'})^2 - 1] with J<0 (favoring orthogonal nearest-neighbor orientations). Monte Carlo simulations are performed on three-dimensional cubic lattices. The J<0 model has a nonfrustrated, extensively degenerate ground state. The conventional J>0 nematic in d>=3 shows a first-order transition. What is the ORDER of the finite-temperature phase transition of the P^2 nematic model with J<0 on a three-dimensional cubic lattice? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: P^2 nematic model with J<0 (orthogonally-favoring interaction) on 3D cubic lattice
metadata.source_excerpt: Monte Carlo simulations on three-dimensional cubic lattices give clear evidence of a finite-temperature phase transition... demonstrating a finite-temperature, symmetry-breaking, continuous transition with sublattice order
metadata.difficulty_note: Contrast with J>0 case which is first-order; J<0 is second-order due to symmetry of sublattice order parameter excluding cubic invariant in Landau expansion.
